file_name: Array BioPharma Inc. - LICENSE, DEVELOPMENT AND COMMERCIALIZATION AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: Array shall (a) cooperate fully with Ono to make available for the benefit of Ono the benefits of Array's supply agreements and/or arrangements with its Third Party suppliers of Materials, and (b) administer such agreements or arrangements diligently and pursue its rights and remedies thereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Array shall (a) consider cooperating with Ono to make available the potential benefits of Array's supply agreements and/or arrangements with its Third Party suppliers of Materials, and (b) administer such agreements or arrangements while considering the pursuit of its rights and remedies thereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The changes from 'shall cooperate fully' to 'consider cooperating,' 'benefits of' to 'potential benefits,' 'administer diligently' to 'administer considering the pursuit' weaken Array's obligation to assist Ono with supply agreements. This introduces ambiguity, making the level of required cooperation unclear and discretionary.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification contradicts the spirit of good faith and commercial reasonableness required in performance of a contract under UCC § 2-311. By making cooperation discretionary, Array could undermine Ono's reasonable expectations regarding material supply.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-311. Options and Cooperation Respecting Performance. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-311. Options and Cooperation Respecting Performance. (1) An agreement for sale which is otherwise sufficiently definite (subsection (3) of Section 2-204 ) to be a contract is not made invalid by the fact that it leaves particulars of performance to be specified by one of the parties. Any such specification must be made in good faith and within limits set by commercial reasonableness. (2) Unless otherwise agreed specifications relating to assortment of the goods are at the buyer's option and except as otherwise provided in subsections (1)(c) and (3) of Section 2-319 specifications or arrangements relating to shipment are at the seller's option. (3)Where such specification would materially affect the other party's performance but is not seasonably made or where one party's cooperation is necessary to the agreed performance of the other but is not seasonably forthcoming, the other party in addition to all other remedies (a) is excused for any resulting delay in his own performance; and (b) may also either proceed to perform in any reasonable manner or after the time for a material part of his own performance treat the failure to specify or to cooperate as a breach by failure to deliver or accept the goods . Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces ambiguity and weakens Array's obligation. The original text states Array 'shall cooperate fully,' while the changed text says Array shall 'consider cooperating.' UCC § 2-311 emphasizes 'good faith' and 'commercial reasonableness.' By making cooperation discretionary, Array's commitment is less certain, contradicting the expectation of good faith in performance, as explained in the Law Explanation. The snippet states that 'where one party's cooperation is necessary to the agreed performance of the other but is not seasonably forthcoming, the other party in addition to all other remedies (a) is excused for any resulting delay in his own performance,' suggesting that a failure to cooperate can lead to excused performance. Therefore, weakening the obligation to cooperate creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly following Ono's request, Array shall transfer, or cause to be transferred, to Ono or such Subcontractor all Array's Know-How that is necessary, useful or actually used for such Manufacture, packaging and/ or testing and release of Materials (and the cost of such transfer of Array Know-How shall be borne by Ono), and shall make personnel of Array reasonably available to assist Ono and/or its contractor in implementing the Array Know-How necessary to Manufacture and/or control and release such Materials.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon Ono's request, Array will endeavor to transfer, or cause to be transferred, to Ono or such Subcontractor Array's Know-How deemed appropriate for such Manufacture, packaging and/ or testing, and may make personnel of Array available, as reasonably practical, to assist Ono and/or its contractor in implementing Array Know-How for Manufacture and/or control and release of such Materials.
perturbation.explanation: The changes from 'Promptly following Ono's request' to 'Upon Ono's request,' 'shall transfer' to 'will endeavor to transfer,' 'necessary, useful or actually used' to 'deemed appropriate,' and 'shall make personnel...reasonably available' to 'may make personnel...available, as reasonably practical' weaken Array's obligation to transfer know-how. This creates ambiguity around Array's commitment to facilitate Ono's manufacturing capabilities.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification introduces ambiguity that undermines compliance with FDA regulations regarding manufacturing (21 CFR § 211.25), which requires that employees have the 'education, training, and experience' to perform their assigned functions. By weakening the know-how transfer, the contract risks that Ono's personnel will not have sufficient training and knowledge, and therefore will be in violation of 21 CFR § 211.25

The United States Code contains only the currently enacted statutory language. The official United States Code is maintained by the Office of the Law Revision Counsel in the United States House of Representatives. The Office of the Law Revision Counsel reviews enacted laws and determines where the statutory language should be codified related to its topic. The Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act and subsequent amending statutes are codified into Title 21 Chapter 9 of the United States Code. perturbation.justification: The modified contract introduces ambiguity regarding the transfer of know-how, potentially undermining compliance with manufacturing regulations. The original text states "shall transfer", whereas the changed text states "will endeavor to transfer", weakening the obligation. The original text also uses language such as "necessary, useful or actually used" which is changed to "deemed appropriate", creating an ambiguity. According to the Law Explanation, this ambiguity undermines compliance with FDA regulations (21 CFR § 211.25) that mandates personnel have adequate training. The scraped snippet 1 from 21 CFR 211.25 supports the argument that personnel qualifications are required by law. The changes in the contract weaken the assurance that Ono's personnel will receive sufficient training, potentially leading to non-compliance, creating a legal contradiction as defined by the 'Ambiguities Legal Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: If Array determines not to file within any jurisdiction requested by Ono, not to continue the prosecution of, or not to continue to maintain or defend, any Array Patent in any country in the Ono Territory, or if Array otherwise determines to abandon any such Array Patent, Array shall promptly notify Ono of such determination sufficiently in advance to enable Ono to undertake or continue the prosecution, maintenance or defense of such Array Patent without a loss of rights, and Ono shall have the right to undertake or continue such prosecution, maintenance or defense at its sole cost and expense.
perturbation.changed_text: If Array considers not filing within any jurisdiction requested by Ono, not continuing the prosecution of, or not continuing to maintain or defend, any Array Patent in any country in the Ono Territory, or if Array otherwise considers abandoning any such Array Patent, Array will attempt to notify Ono of such consideration in advance, and Ono may have the right to undertake or continue such prosecution, maintenance or defense at its cost.
perturbation.explanation: The changes from 'determines not to' to 'considers not,' 'shall promptly notify' to 'will attempt to notify,' and 'shall have the right' to 'may have the right' weaken Array's obligation to notify Ono of patent abandonment. This introduces ambiguity and makes it less certain that Ono will be able to protect its interests.
perturbation.law_explanation: The added ambiguity could lead to Array not properly informing Ono of an issue with a patent. If a patent requires reissuance due to an error, and Ono isn't properly notified, it may be in violation of 35 U.S. Code § 251 if they attempt to reissue the patent themselves, given Array's responsibility for maintaining the patent. By limiting the responsibility to act with urgency, the duty of reissuing the patent is put in question.

perturbation.justification: The original contract uses 'shall promptly notify', indicating a clear obligation to inform Ono. The modified text uses 'will attempt to notify', significantly weakening this obligation. Similarly, 'shall have the right' is changed to 'may have the right', diluting Ono's entitlement. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities Legal Contradiction' definition, where vague language makes obligations difficult to enforce. The Law Explanation suggests that this ambiguity could lead to non-compliance regarding patent reissuance under 35 U.S. Code § 251. Scraped Law Snippet 1 references 35 USC 251, dealing with the reissue of defective patents, suggesting that the weakening of Array's obligation to notify Ono could negatively impact Ono's ability to act within the bounds of the law should a patent require reissuance.
